summary: PVP Mode Bug Fix - Fixed critical datetime serialization bug preventing game_start WebSocket broadcast. The root cause was session.model_dump() returning datetime objects that are not JSON serializable, causing broadcast_game_start to fail silently. Fixed by using model_dump(mode='json') which serializes datetime to ISO strings.

fixed.issue: game_start WebSocket message was not being broadcast due to datetime serialization error
fixed.fix: Changed session.model_dump() to session.model_dump(mode='json') in game_routes.py lines 589-590 and 599

critical_code_review_comments: The datetime serialization issue was found in backend logs: 'WARNING:game_routes:Could not broadcast game start: Object of type datetime is not JSON serializable'
PVPGameSession model has datetime fields (created_at, completed_at) that need JSON serialization when broadcasting via WebSocket
context_for_next_testing_agent: PVP mode is now working. The fix ensures game_start WebSocket message is broadcast with pvp_room_id. Full 2-player flow tested: create game -> join game -> both ready -> countdown -> start game -> WebSocket game_start received -> PVP room WebSocket connection successful.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: session.model_dump() returns datetime objects for created_at and completed_at fields. When broadcast_game_start tried to JSON serialize the session_data, it failed with 'Object of type datetime is not JSON serializable'
reproduction_steps: 6. broadcast_game_start fails silently due to datetime serialization
7. Frontend never receives game_start message with pvp_room_id
8. PVPBattleArena cannot connect to WebSocket without pvp_room_id
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Changed model_dump() to model_dump(mode='json') which uses Pydantic's JSON serialization mode that converts datetime objects to ISO format strings
